name: EPA+DHA reduced chronic venous ulcer PMN activation with wound-area trends
summary: In a chronic venous leg ulcer randomized study, EPA+DHA lowered activated PMNs in wound fluid and showed larger wound-area reductions than control, but the staged interpretation treats this as biomarker-heavy support.
statement: In chronic venous leg ulcer patients receiving standard care, EPA+DHA supplementation reduced the percentage of CD66b+ activated PMNs in wound fluid by day 56 and showed greater wound-area reduction than control at day 28 and day 56, with wound-area reduction inversely related to PMN and activated PMN markers.
population_or_model: Patients with chronic venous leg ulcers receiving standard compression and wound care
quantitative_note: The source reports wound-area reductions of 57% and 76% in EPA+DHA at days 28 and 56 versus 35% and 59% in control, but the staged rating does not treat this as definitive human functional benefit.
caveats: The finding is from a small pilot study.
The clearest endpoint is wound-fluid inflammatory activity rather than a prespecified definitive healing outcome.
EPA+DHA may act through broader omega-3 mechanisms, not only SPM generation.
